title: eriogonum trichopes subsp. clavatum
intro: Eriogonum trichopes subsp. clavatum, also known as California buckwheat, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the buckwheat family (Polygonaceae). It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to treat wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive issues, as well as its potential to lower blood sugar levels and reduce inflammation. Eriogonum trichopes subsp. clavatum can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures for medicinal use, and it is also used in traditional folk remedies for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties.
